thank you for sharing, but the science is incorrect. Nail polish or acetone doesn’t float because of surface tension alone; it floats because it is less dense than water and doesn’t mix well with it, while surface tension helps maintain a thin film at the interface. Similarly, the fire is not “kept floating by surface tension”. What actually happens is that the acetone layer on top of the water burns, since acetone is volatile and flammable in the presence of oxygen. So please note that density and immiscibility explain the floating, and flammability explains the burning, while surface tension plays only a supporting role in stabilizing the thin layer. please see our website for a simple activity on surface tension: ,[object Object],
